item.title: Wildfire season is getting ugly. Weather is only part of the story.
item.description: A complex maze of rules and regulations is blocking a bipartisan effort to manage American forests.

item.title: Is there such a thing as too much empathy?
item.description: Is empathy destroying the West? That's the provocative claim at the center of Gad Saad's new book, "Suicidal Empathy." Host Megan McArdle is joined by Saad, a professor at the University of Mississippi, to discuss the trade-offs societies make in the name of compassion and whether America has the capacity to absorb differences.

item.title: Lock them up? Nixon’s war on drugs wasn’t what you think.
item.description: With opioids ravaging communities, the U.S. could learn from this 1971 policy.

item.title: What a 150-year-old oak teaches about Juneteenth
item.description: A federal holiday survives, but a national custom has yet to take root.

item.title: Taxing the rich won’t save Social Security
item.description: Bernie Sanders wants a massive tax hike for the wrong priorities.

item.title: Building new homes doesn’t have to be hard
item.description: Permitting reform for housing has amassed enormous bipartisan appeal.

item.title: What happens next in Iran?
item.description: What is the state of Iranian society following years of extreme tension? In this episode of "Make It Make Sense," Opinion Editor Adam O'Neal sits down with Jason Rezaian, The Washington Post's director of press freedom initiatives, to examine the devastating impact of conflict on Iran's citizens and why the promise of liberation remains unfulfilled.

item.title: What success in Iran means for Trump — and Netanyahu
item.description: Presidential spats with Israeli prime ministers are nothing new.

item.title: The NRA is almost out of bullets
item.description: The legal battle facing the gun rights organization is an existential threat.

item.title: This common women’s health issue is getting a new name. It’s about time.
item.description: Polycystic ovary syndrome was always a misnomer. Its new name will help women get proper treatment.

item.title: Good luck, JD Vance. I sense a setup.
item.description: The vice president is tasked with helping the Iran regime “learn the ways of peace.”

item.title: How the Trump administration built its exit ramp in Iran
item.description: Kushner, Witkoff and a Qatari mediator turned an investor pitch into a fragile peace deal.

item.title: The nest egg
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on Social Security solvency.

item.title: Why Gen Z hates capitalism
item.description: Has Gen Z completely given up on capitalism — and America? In this episode of "Make It Make Sense," James Hohmann sits down with columnist and “Reasonably Optimistic” host Megan McArdle to explore why young voters are turning to socialism and how politicians like Zohran Mamdani are capitalizing on their angst.

item.title: Switzerland says ‘no’ to anti-immigration insanity
item.description: Voters reject a cap on the nation’s population.

item.title: Putin calls Zelensky illegitimate. He’s describing himself.
item.description: The Russian dictator’s obsession reveals exactly what occupies his own subconscious.

item.title: Here’s the real Democratic autopsy
item.description: Why did the party lose in ’24? Neither Jill Biden’s memoir nor the DNC’s postmortem has the answer.
